articles.title: 'Dhurandhar lawyers must ensure punishment for paper-leak accused': PM Modi
How 'sluggish' initial response may have led to Monday's mess at CJP-led protest
articles.description: Police faced criticism for inaction during the recent 'Sandad Chalo' march.  Intelligence reports provided advance warning of the large demonstrator build-up.  Officers reported a lack of clear orders during the crucial initial hour.  This hesitation allowed crowds to breach key security perimeters and reach Parliament.  Similar administrative hesitations occurred during past protests at Red Fort and India Gate.

articles.title: A fragile fitness system? Hamstring injuries put Team India, CoE under scanner
articles.description: The recurring injuries suffered by Harshit Rana and Nitish Kumar Reddy have not gone down well with the BCCI. Sources said Reddy was being pushed to bowl close to 140kmph to compensate for the injury-prone Hardik Pandya. During Rahul Dravid’s tenure as head coach, the BCCI medical team, the CoE and the team management had charted out year-long roadmaps for individual players around major ICC events. The objective was to get each player to the required fitness level in time for the next ICC tournament or marquee Test series.

articles.title: Gold, silver rate live updates today: Global cues, Fed outlook and geopolitics set to steer gold, silver prices
articles.full_text: Silver attracted investor interest after analysts attributed its gains to improving expectations for industrial demand. Hopes that diplomatic engagement between the United States and Iran could reduce regional tensions supported broader market sentiment, while China's efforts to stabilise its technology sector through large-scale state-backed investments also lifted confidence in industrial metals. As a result, silver has outperformed gold in international markets, with prices registering stronger percentage gains. The metal's dual role as both a precious and industrial commodity continued to make it sensitive to changes in manufacturing activity and broader economic expectations. Investors continued to monitor the US Federal Reserve's policy outlook for fresh clues on the future direction of interest rates. According to a Reuters poll, economists expect the central bank to keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged for the remainder of 2026 as it continues its efforts to tackle persistent inflation. Interest rate expectations remain one of the most significant influences on gold prices because lower borrowing costs generally improve the appeal of non-yielding assets such as bullion. With markets awaiting further policy signals, traders remained focused on incoming economic data and central bank commentary. Market participants closely tracked diplomatic developments involving the United States and Iran as negotiations influenced broader commodity markets. According to analysts, optimism surrounding renewed diplomatic efforts reduced concerns over an immediate escalation in geopolitical tensions, contributing to a pullback in crude oil prices. Despite easing tensions, bullion continued to attract buying interest, with gold extending its gains. Investors remained cautious as developments in the Middle East continued to evolve, highlighting the complex relationship between geopolitical risks, energy markets and demand for safe-haven assets such as gold. Analysts pointed to sustained physical demand from China and continued purchases by central banks as two of the biggest factors supporting gold prices. Even as global markets responded to geopolitical and macroeconomic developments, underlying demand for bullion remained resilient. Central bank buying has remained an important pillar for gold over recent months, helping offset periods of market volatility. At the same time, healthy demand from China, one of the world's largest consumers of gold, continued to provide a firm foundation for prices. These structural demand drivers have kept sentiment positive even during periods of profit booking and corrections in international markets. Gold prices in the national capital gained Rs 800 per 10 grams on Tuesday as strong physical buying and favourable global cues lifted sentiment in the bullion market. The price of 99.9 per cent purity gold climbed to Rs 1,47,700 per 10 grams, inclusive of taxes, from the previous close of Rs 1,46,900. Traders attributed the rise to healthy domestic demand as buyers continued to accumulate the yellow metal despite elevated price levels. The domestic rally also mirrored strength in international markets, where gold prices advanced sharply. In contrast, silver prices remained unchanged at Rs 2,21,500 per kilogram, reflecting relatively subdued activity after the recent correction in the white metal.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: BCI draft Bill seeks higher enrolment fee, law admission changes and professional development programmes
articles.description: The Bar Council of India has proposed raising the statutory advocate enrolment fee from Rs 750 to Rs 22,500 through the Draft Advocates (Amendment) Bill, 2026. The draft also recommends entrance examinations for law admissions, changes to the All India Bar Examination, expansion of the Legal Education Committee and mandatory continuing legal education. Stakeholders, including law universities and bar associations, can submit suggestions to the BCI until 3 pm on July 31, 2026.
articles.full_text: The Bar Council of India has proposed raising the statutory advocate enrolment fee from Rs 750 to Rs 22,500 through the Draft Advocates (Amendment) Bill, 2026. The draft also recommends entrance examinations for law admissions, changes to the All India Bar Examination, expansion of the Legal Education Committee and mandatory continuing legal education. Stakeholders, including law universities and bar associations, can submit suggestions to the BCI until 3 pm on July 31, 2026.

articles.title: Dharmendra Pradhan says government ready for NEET debate, criticises Congress protest
articles.description: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan has accused Rahul Gandhi and the Congress of politicising the NEET issue during the Monsoon Session of Parliament. In a post on X, he said the government was ready for a comprehensive discussion on the examination but alleged that the Congress chose protest over parliamentary debate. The minister reiterated that the government remains committed to addressing students' concerns through discussion, reforms and accountability.
articles.full_text: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan has accused Rahul Gandhi and the Congress of politicising the NEET issue during the Monsoon Session of Parliament. In a post on X, he said the government was ready for a comprehensive discussion on the examination but alleged that the Congress chose protest over parliamentary debate. The minister reiterated that the government remains committed to addressing students' concerns through discussion, reforms and accountability.

articles.title: UDF urges Centre to establish independent examination authority and reform NEET system
articles.description: The United Doctors Front has submitted a representation to the President of India and the Prime Minister seeking the replacement of the National Testing Agency with an independent statutory National Examination Authority created through an Act of Parliament. The organisation has also sought institutional reforms, protection of medical personnel during public gatherings, dialogue with NEET aspirants, lawful examination of complaints and measures to improve transparency, accountability and public confidence in national entrance examinations.

articles.title: RRB JE Recruitment 2026 notification released for 4,098 technical posts with applications starting August 14; check details here
articles.description: RRB JE Recruitment 2026: The Railway Recruitment Boards have released CEN No. 04/2026 for recruitment to 4,098 Junior Engineer, Depot Material Superintendent and Chemical & Metallurgical Assistant posts. Online applications are scheduled to open on August 14, 2026, and close on September 13, 2026. Candidates aged 18 to 33 years with the required degree can apply online. The selection process is expected to include two CBTs, document verification and a medical examination.

The selection process is expected to include two CBTs, document verification and a medical examination.

articles.title: Sunidhi Chauhan opens up on quitting reality shows
articles.description: Singer Sunidhi Chauhan revealed on 'Shekhar Tonite' why she quit judging reality shows, saying, "Mujhe laga yeh meri jagah nahin hai," citing the blurred line between music production and reality TV. She also shared she's never faced negative behavior from audiences during live performances, only love and respect.

articles.title: Ranbir off to US for 'Ramayana' launch at Comic-Con - WATCH
articles.description: Bollywood star Ranbir Kapoor is traveling to San Diego Comic-Con for the film's global launch. The epic mythological film 'Ramayana' will have a special presentation on July 23. Exclusive footage and the official trailer will be revealed at the event. This marks a significant step in the film's international promotional campaign. The ambitious project is slated for a two-part release in 2026 and 2027.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Chris Martin
articles.description: Chris Martin's words from Coldplay's 'Adventure of a Lifetime' encapsulate his transformative experiences. The diamond metaphor represents how he thrived under immense pressure, reflecting a personal evolution following his split from Gwyneth Paltrow. This challenging time gave rise to his album 'Ghost Stories,' which explores themes of anxiety and heartache, allowing Martin to find comfort in literature and poetry.

articles.title: Zeeshan denies involvement in Ajay Devgn's Chauhaan
articles.description: Mohd Zeeshan Ayyub clarified he has no connection to Ajay Devgn's 'Chauhaan', explaining he'd dubbed a line "without any reference." His voice was later removed after the teaser sparked backlash from the Chauhan community and Kshatriya Parishad. The film, reportedly inspired by a Jama Masjid incident, releases October 1, 2027.
